what_it_does: Browser-side model fetcher that reads provider API keys from localStorage, calls each provider's /models endpoint when possible, classifies returned models by kind and tier, and falls back to a curated registry on failure or missing key.
observations: Uses an IIFE attached to window.MS_FETCH.
Contains per-provider regex-based classifiers for kind and tier.
Anthropic fetcher anticipates browser CORS blocking and provides a specific fallback message.
Ollama defaults to http://localhost:11434 and expects a local server.
The classify object and annotate function are exposed publicly.
Keys are stored with the ms_key_<provider> prefix.
Google passes the API key as a query parameter; OpenAI uses Bearer; Anthropic uses x-api-key.
annotate preserves any existing kind or tier fields on model objects.
standouts: Regex-heavy, hand-curated classification table for OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and Ollama.
Explicit CORS-aware handling for Anthropic with a tailored fallback note.
Live fetch plus curated registry fallback pattern for every provider.
conventions: Per-provider classifier functions grouped in a classify object.
concerns: Regex order matters and overlapping patterns make the classifier fragile to future model IDs.
Hard-coded provider endpoints and Anthropic API version string.
classify.google uses a broad /image/ substring that may match non-image model IDs.
No validation or normalization of the Ollama baseUrl parameter.
Relies on global window.MS_DATA without a guard.
Error messages mix user-facing text with implementation-specific references like 'Studio backend proxy'.
describeNetworkError only recognizes the literal 'Failed to fetch' message.
notes: This is the client-side adapter that turns provider API keys into a normalized, classified model list for the Settings UI.
questions: What is the exact shape of window.MS_DATA.providers and its model entries?
Where is the Settings UI that consumes these classified models?
Is there a server-side proxy for Anthropic implemented elsewhere?
How frequently are models fetched and is there any caching?
Who populates the localStorage ms_key_<provider> entries?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—fetch provider model lists from the browser and classify them for the Settings UI—matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Browser-first direct API access with CORS awareness
intention_signals: The file is trying to let the app present an up-to-date, scoped model picker without requiring a backend, while still having a curated fallback when live fetching is impossible.
novelty: The explicit, provider-specific regex taxonomy mapping raw model IDs to UI kind/tier is a distinct hand-curated classification layer rather than relying on provider metadata.
contribution: A self-contained, browser-runnable fetcher plus a heuristic model classifier that can be reused or replaced independently; it contributes the live-vs-registry fallback pattern and the kind/tier annotation logic.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-side provider model fetcher with regex-based kind/tier classification and curated registry fallback.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the Settings UI model list current by fetching provider endpoints directly and classifying results for scoping.

symbols.does: Retrieves a provider API key from localStorage.
symbols.description: Reads the stored key using the ms_key_<provider> prefix and returns an empty string if missing; used by each live fetcher to decide whether to attempt a request.

symbols.does: Adds kind and tier annotations to model objects.
symbols.description: Maps each model through the provider-specific classifier while preserving any existing kind or tier fields, returning enriched model objects for the UI.
Calls https://api.openai.com/v1/models with a Bearer token, normalizes the response, annotates each model, and falls back to the curated registry on error or missing key.

symbols.does: Fetches and classifies Google Gemini models.
symbols.description: Calls the Generative Language API v1beta models endpoint using a query-parameter key, maps names to IDs, and applies Google-specific kind/tier classification.
Calls https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models with x-api-key and anthropic-version headers; on failure it specifically reports CORS blocking and falls back to the registry.

symbols.does: Fetches and classifies local Ollama models.
symbols.description: Hits the Ollama /api/tags endpoint at a configurable base URL, defaults to localhost:11434, and classifies local model names by capability.
Looks up the provider in the global registry, clones and annotates its model list, and marks the result as source: 'registry' with a note explaining why live fetch failed.

symbols.does: Translates a network exception into a short message.
symbols.description: Detects the generic 'Failed to fetch' message and reports it as a likely CORS block; otherwise returns the exception message.

symbols.does: Formats a raw model ID into a readable name.
symbols.description: Replaces provider prefixes and hyphens with spaces and title-cases the result, used when no display name is provided.
